senses.definition: to have a sexually attractive appearance

examples.x: By next month, she'll have got it going on even more, enhancing her natural beauty and radiating confidence.
She is currently having it going on, turning heads wherever she goes with her stunning looks and poise.

senses.definition: the most attractive or popular woman at a party, dance, or any other social event

examples.x: Last night, she was the belle of the ball, enchanting everyone with her stunning gown and captivating smile.
They are the belles of the ball, radiating beauty and elegance as they dance.

word_list.pronunciation: lˈʊk fˈiːl lˈaɪk ɐ mˈɪliən dˈɑːlɚz

senses.definition: to look very attractive, well-dressed, and stylish, as if one had spent a lot of money on their appearance

examples.x: He feels a million dollars as he walks down the street, receiving compliments on his stylish appearance.
On his wedding day, he looked like a million dollars in his tailored tuxedo and beaming smile.
She looks a million dollars in her elegant evening gown and flawless makeup.
They feel like a million dollars when they dress up for a special occasion and receive admiring glances.

senses.definition: to attract a lot of interest or attention because of how great someone or something looks

examples.x: During their performance, they turned heads with their exceptional talent and stage presence.
He turns heads when he walks into a room, with his charismatic personality and fashionable attire.
The sleek sports car turns heads as it zooms down the street, with its roaring engine and eye-catching design.

senses.definition: used to describe someone who has tanned skin, due to prolonged exposure to the sunlight

examples.x: After a week of camping under the clear skies, swimming in the lake, and enjoying outdoor activities, he was brown as a berry, his skin bronzed from the sun's warm embrace.
She is as brown as a berry after spending the entire summer at the beach, soaking up the sun.

senses.definition: an appearance that is not perfectly organized, neat, and tidy

examples.x: He always ensures that his outfit is impeccable, never a hair out of place.
She never has a hair out of place, with her meticulously styled hair and polished appearance.
They presented themselves flawlessly at the ceremony, not a hair out of place.

senses.definition: used to refer to someone's hair

examples.x: Her long, flowing locks were her crowning glory, earning her compliments wherever she went.
Despite her advancing age, her silver hair remained her crowning glory, adding an air of elegance to her appearance.
The bride's intricate updo adorned with flowers was the perfect crowning glory for her wedding day.
The actress's glossy, voluminous curls were styled to perfection, serving as her crowning glory on the red carpet.
The salon's expertly crafted hairstyles became the town's crowning glory, attracting clients from far and wide.

senses.definition: a person whose hair has a color between red and brown

examples.x: The actress, known for her carrot top, rose to fame in the entertainment industry and left a lasting impression on audiences.
The girl with the carrot top confidently embraces her unique hair color, radiating a sense of individuality.

senses.definition: a day on which one feels unattractive, particularly due to one's hair not looking as well as it should

examples.x: During the event last week, he had a bad hair day and regretted not scheduling a salon appointment beforehand.
She's having a bad hair day today, as her usually sleek and shiny locks are frizzy and unmanageable.

senses.definition: to stop caring for one's physical or mental health

examples.x: She is letting herself go by neglecting her exercise routine and indulging in unhealthy eating habits, which is affecting her overall well-being.

senses.definition: (of appearance) looking untidy, rough, or disorganized

examples.x: Her hair is wild and woolly today, as if she just woke up.
The garden appears wild and woolly with overgrown bushes and tangled vines.
The dog's fur gets wild and woolly if not groomed regularly.
The construction site looks wild and woolly with scattered tools and materials.
After a long hike, their clothes were wild and woolly, covered in dirt and sweat.
If the wind continues, my hair will be wild and woolly by the time we arrive.

word_list.pronunciation: lˈʊk fˈiːl lˈaɪk sˈʌmθɪŋ ðə kˈæt bɹˈɪŋ dɹˈæɡ ˈɪn

senses.definition: to look very disorganized and untidy

examples.x: He looks like something the cat brought in with his disheveled hair and dirty clothes.
She looks like something the cat dragged in after a long night of partying, with smudged makeup and tired eyes.
They feel like something the cat dragged in after a strenuous workout, sore and fatigued.
Yesterday, she had felt like something the cat brought in after staying up all night studying, mentally drained and sleep-deprived.
